# {{< i18n requirements_doc >}} {}

## Purpose of This Document

This document defines the **project requirements baseline**, capturing:

- Business requirements
- Environmental and ecological requirements
- Functional requirements
- Non-functional requirements
- Governance and compliance requirements

It is a **mandatory PMBOK Planning artifact** and must be approved **before
finalization of the Project Management Plan (PMP)**.

---

## Requirements Guiding Principles

1. **Ecological protection is the primary requirement**
2. Automation must not compromise environmental confidence
3. Human-in-the-loop validation is mandatory
4. Requirements must be:
   - Verifiable
   - Traceable
   - Testable
5. No requirement is valid unless it supports **[OKR at section @sec-okr]**

---

## Business Requirements (BR)

| ID | Requirement | Rationale | Acceptance Criteria |
|-----|---------------|------------|-----------------|
| BR-01 | Reduce manual screening effort for AUV imagery | Scale ecological analysis | ≥ 30% reduction in manual screening volume |
| BR-02 | Reduce time to environmental insight | Faster ecological decision-making | ≥ 25% reduction vs baseline |
| BR-03 | Preserve ecological confidence | Avoid loss of benthic data | ≥ 85–90% retention of true benthic imagery |
| BR-04 | Enable reuse across fields | Dilute cost and effort | Demonstrated reuse in ≥ 2 fields |
| BR-05 | Create reusable digital environmental assets | Long-term value creation | Assets versioned and auditable |

---

## Environmental & Ecological Requirements (ER)

| ID | Requirement | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
|-----|-------------|--------------------|----------------|
| ER-01 | Benthic relevance protection | Screening must prioritize retention of benthic-relevant imagery | KPI-3 ≥ threshold |
| ER-02 | Human ecological authority | Final validation performed by environmental SMEs | SME sign-off |
| ER-03 | Transparency of limitations | Model assumptions and limits documented | Validation report approved |
| ER-04 | Consistency across fields | Ecological interpretation must be stable | KPI-4 within tolerance |
| ER-05 | Auditability | Ecological decisions traceable | Full data lineage |

---

## Functional Requirements (FR)

### Data Ingestion

| ID | Requirement | Description |
|----|---------|-------------------|
| FR-01 | AUV image ingestion | Ingest still images from AUV campaigns |
| FR-02 | Metadata ingestion | Capture temporal, spatial, and campaign metadata |
| FR-03 | Batch processing | Support offline, batch-oriented workflows |

---

### Preprocessing

| ID | Requirement | Description |
|----|---------|------------------|
| FR-04 | Image normalization | Normalize resolution and basic visual properties |
| FR-05 | Quality filtering | Identify unusable frames (blur, severe turbidity) |

---

### Annotation & Validation

| ID | Requirement | Description |
|----|---------|------------------|
| FR-06 | AI-assisted annotation | Support assisted labeling workflows |
| FR-07 | Human-in-the-loop | Enable SME validation and override |
| FR-08 | Iterative refinement | Support retraining based on SME feedback |

---

### Model Training & Inference

| ID | Requirement | Description |
|----|-----------|------------------|
| FR-09 | Binary classification | Classify images as benthic-relevant or not |
| FR-10 | Precision-oriented inference | Prioritize false-negative control |
| FR-11 | Explainability support | Provide traceable outputs |

---

### Output & Asset Management

| ID | Requirement | Description |
|----|-----------|------------------|
| FR-12 | Filtered dataset output | Produce curated datasets |
| FR-13 | Versioning | Version datasets and models |
| FR-14 | Traceability | Maintain lineage and audit trails |

---

## Non-Functional Requirements (NFR)

| ID | Requirement | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
|-----|----------|----------------|---------------------|
| NFR-01 | Scalability | Handle TB-scale datasets | Successful batch processing |
| NFR-02 | Offline-first | No dependency on real-time systems | Offline execution validated |
| NFR-03 | Performance | Process within acceptable time | Meets KPI-2 |
| NFR-04 | Reliability | Stable repeated runs | Consistent outputs |
| NFR-05 | Security | Access-controlled data | Petrobras IT compliance |
| NFR-06 | Maintainability | Modular pipeline | Code review approval |

---

## Compliance & Governance Requirements (CR)

| ID | Requirement | Description |
|----|---------|------------------|
| CR-01 | Licensing compliance | Use only license-compliant AI frameworks |
| CR-02 | Data governance | Align with Petrobras data governance |
| CR-03 | Auditability | Full traceability for audits |
| CR-04 | Environmental governance | Alignment with SUB environmental policies |
| CR-05 | Stage-Gate adherence | Subject to KPI-based gates |

---

## Requirements Traceability (High-Level)

Each requirement must be traceable to:

- [OKR at section @sec-okr]
- At least one executive KPI
- One or more WBS elements (defined later)

A formal **Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM)** will be created during PMP
development.

---

## Assumptions and Constraints (Reference)

- No pre-labeled dataset initially available
- Natural class imbalance exists
- Offline-first constraint applies
- Environmental SMEs availability assumed

*See Assumption Log and Risk Register (@sec-risk-register) for details.*

---

## Approval and Baseline

This Requirements Specification becomes **baselined** upon approval.  
Changes require formal change control.
